quote: I already feel watched in the LMS; please don't touch my email metadata.
If the outreach is supportive and optional, I'm okay with it—just don't mislabel me because I work nights.
Early pings at week 3 could help us reach students before they disappear.
False positives are a concern; we need a human in the loop before any outreach.
We will not ingest subject lines or bodies; only to/from, timestamp, and thread count.
Section-level normalization is essential so heavy-LMS courses don’t bias the risk score.
Advisors qualify as school officials with a legitimate educational interest when acting to support student success.
Don’t send me alerts unless a student opts in or asks me to be looped in.
Avoid proxies that penalize students who use extended-time accommodations.
Email patterns differ across time zones; timestamp logic must account for that.
